skill.name: chat-ui
skill.description: Build React and Next.js chat interfaces with shadcn/ui-style components for containers, messages, inputs, typing indicators, and avatars.
skill.summary: Chat UI building blocks for React and Next.js applications.
skill.author: inference-sh-9

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported Ruby backtick execution and weak cryptography, but review found these are false positives from Markdown fences, JSX examples, table text, and front matter. The real risk is a supply-chain warning: the skill instructs users to run npx commands and fetch components from hardcoded external URLs.

medium_findings.title: Remote Package Installation Instructions
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs users to run npx commands that install components or skills from external sources. This is expected for UI component setup, but users should verify the remote registry and package contents before running the commands.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The npx commands are directly visible in shell examples and fetch from external sources. They are documentation examples rather than hidden execution, so the issue is supply-chain risk rather than confirmed malware.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded External Documentation and Asset URLs
low_findings.description: The skill references external URLs for documentation, an image, and component registry content. These links match the stated ui.inference.sh source and do not show evidence of credential collection or data exfiltration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are plain Markdown references and one documented registry URL. No evidence found that the skill sends local data, secrets, or user content to those endpoints.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby backtick execution and weak cryptographic algorithm findings are not supported by context. The cited lines contain Markdown fences, JSX snippets, role names, or prose, with no executable Ruby code or cryptographic implementation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding context is visible and consists of Markdown documentation, TSX examples, and shell snippets. No evidence found of Ruby execution, MD5, SHA1, or other weak cryptographic code.

dangerous_patterns.title: npx Command Fetches Remote Registry Content
dangerous_patterns.description: The setup command pulls a chat component definition from a hardcoded remote URL. This pattern can introduce unreviewed third-party code into a project if the remote source changes.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command and URL are directly present in the quick start instructions. The behavior is transparent and user-initiated, which lowers concern compared with hidden execution.

content.value_statement: Teams need consistent chat interfaces for assistants, messaging, and streaming conversations. This skill provides React and Next.js chat component patterns with shadcn/ui styling guidance.
actual_capabilities: Shows how to install chat components with a shadcn registry command.
Documents ChatContainer, ChatMessage, ChatInput, and TypingIndicator usage.
Provides a complete React chat example with message state and loading state.
Explains message role variants for user, assistant, and system messages.
Links to related documentation for agent chat interfaces and streaming.
limitations: Does not include the component source code in this skill file.
Requires React, Next.js, Tailwind CSS, and shadcn/ui conventions.
Does not implement backend message persistence or model API calls.
Uses external registry and documentation links that should be reviewed before use.

use_cases.title: Prototype an AI Assistant Chat
use_cases.description: Create a working chat layout with message bubbles, loading state, and a reusable input area.

use_cases.title: Standardize Chat Components
use_cases.description: Align product teams on shared chat containers, message variants, and input patterns.

use_cases.title: Add Streaming Feedback
use_cases.description: Show users that an assistant is responding with a typing indicator and disabled input state.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Chat Layout
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the chat-ui skill to create a simple React chat screen with a container, two messages, and a message input.

prompt_templates.title: Wire Message Submission
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the chat-ui skill to add message state, append user messages, show loading state, and disable input while sending.

prompt_templates.title: Match an Existing Design System
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the chat-ui skill to adapt the chat components to an existing Tailwind and shadcn/ui design system.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Streaming Assistant Flow
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the chat-ui skill to design a streaming assistant chat with user, assistant, system, loading, and empty states.

output_examples.input: Build a support assistant chat for a dashboard.
output_examples.output: A dashboard chat panel with a message container, role-based messages, a disabled input while sending, and a typing indicator.

output_examples.input: Add chat UI patterns to our design system.
output: Component list for chat container, message, input, and typing indicator.
Role guidance for user, assistant, and system messages.
Styling notes that align with Tailwind and shadcn/ui tokens.

output_examples.input: Improve an AI chat prototype for streaming responses.
output_examples.output: A refined conversation flow with loading feedback, stable message layout, and clear input behavior during assistant responses.

best_practices: Review remote registry content before running installation commands.
Keep message state and network request state separate for clearer loading behavior.
Use consistent role styling so users can scan the conversation quickly.
anti_patterns: Do not run remote npx commands in production projects without reviewing the fetched files.
Do not mix backend model calls directly into presentational chat components.
Do not rely only on a typing indicator when errors or timeouts need explicit states.

faq.question: What does this skill help build?
faq.answer: It helps build React and Next.js chat interfaces with reusable component patterns.

faq.question: Does it include backend AI integration?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on UI structure and leaves API calls to the application.

faq.question: Can I use it with Claude or Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill is documented for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows.

faq.question: Does it require shadcn/ui?
faq.answer: It uses shadcn/ui and Tailwind CSS conventions, so those patterns are expected.

faq.question: Why is the security risk medium?
faq.answer: The skill asks users to run remote npx installation commands. This is visible and user-initiated, but it carries supply-chain risk.

faq.question: Should I review anything before using it?
faq.answer: Yes. Review the remote registry files and related skill commands before adding them to a project.
